content.text:
من نمی‌توانم خودم باکس چت را RTL کنم؛ این کار را فقط افزونهٔ **Cursor RTL** داخل خود Cursor انجام می‌دهد. افزونه‌ات نصب است (`motcke.cursor-rtl`) و تنظیماتش هم فعال است. فقط باید **پچ RTL** را روشن کنی: ### قدم‌ها 1. پایین صفحه، سمت راست نوار وضعیت را ببین: باید چیزی مثل **RTL: OFF** یا **RTL: ON** باشد. 2. روی همان **RTL** کلیک کن → **Enable RTL** را بزن. یا `Ctrl+Shift+P` → بنویس: `Cursor RTL: Enable RTL Support` → Enter 3. اگر اجازه خواست، تأیید کن. 4. **همهٔ پنجره‌های Cursor را ببند و دوباره باز کن** (Restart). ### بعد از Restart - نوار وضعیت باید **RTL: ON** باشد. - باکس نوشتن چت هم راست‌به‌چپ می‌شود. اگر بعد از Enable خطا داد (مثلاً دسترسی فایل)، یک‌بار Cursor را **با Run as administrator** باز کن و دوباره Enable بزن — چون پچ روی فایل‌های نصب Cursor اعمال می‌شود. بعد از Restart بگو **RTL: ON** شد یا نه.
[REDACTED]

حق با توست — توی همین صفحه‌ای که الان باز داری، آن «نوار وضعیت RTL» معمولاً **دیده نمی‌شود**. ### چرا نمی‌بینی؟ الان داخل **پنجرهٔ Agents** هستی (چت Agent)، نه داخل **Editor** معمولی پروژه. افزونهٔ Cursor RTL معمولاً نشان **RTL: ON/OFF** را پایینِ **پنجرهٔ Editor** می‌گذارد؛ اینجا فقط چیزهایی مثل `dev` و `This PC` می‌بینی. ### اشتباه دوم در اسکرین‌شات دوم جایی که `RTL` نوشتی، **جستجوی Agents** است (`+ New agent: "RTL"`). این همان Command Palette افزونه‌ها نیست. --- ### همین الان این کار را بکن (بدون نوار وضعیت) 1. کلیدها را بزن: **`Ctrl + Shift + P`** 2. باید جعبه‌ای باز شود که بالای آن معمولاً نوشته شبیه **`>`** دارد (نه تب‌های All / Agents / Files). 3. دقیقاً این را بنویس: ```text Cursor RTL: Enable RTL Support ``` 4. همان گزینه را انتخاب کن → تأیید کن → **همهٔ Cursor را ببند و دوباره باز کن**. --- ### اگر با `Ctrl+Shift+P` آن دستور را ندیدی 1. از منوی بالا برو: **File → Open Folder** 2. پوشهٔ پروژه را باز کن: `D:\Elay_Backend-master` 3. حالا پایینِ همان پنجرهٔ Editor، نوار باریک وضعیت را ببین — آنجا باید **RTL** بیاید. 4. یا دوباره `Ctrl+Shift+P` → `Cursor RTL: Enable RTL Support` --- خلاصه: نوار وضعیت RTL مال این صفحهٔ Agents نیست؛ یا از **`Ctrl+Shift+P`** دستور Enable را بزن، یا یک‌بار پروژه را در **Editor** باز کن. اگر بعد از `Ctrl+Shift+P` هنوز دستور Cursor RTL نبود، بگو تا قدم بعدی (نصب/فعال‌سازی افزونه) را چک کنیم.

عمق فاجعه را از روی خود بک‌اند هم تأیید می‌کنم؛ مشکل فقط Flutter نیست. خلاصهٔ تشخیص و سه طرح اصلاح را می‌دهم. ---
## تشخیص مشکل (از روی کد واقعی) بعد از لاگین، اپ تقریباً یک «دنیای کامل» را می‌کشد تا خودش روی دستگاه تصمیم بگیرد. در بک‌اند این را می‌بینیم: ### ۱) `GET /Sync/Form` — سنگین‌ترین بمب همهٔ `FormTemplate`های فعال + Roles + Positions + **همهٔ فیلدها** را برمی‌گرداند. `ICurrentUserService` تزریق شده ولی **اصلاً استفاده نمی‌شود**؛ یعنی فیلتر کاربر/سمت وجود ندارد. ### ۲) `GET /Sync/Setting` — منبع منطق اشتباه سمت فرانت در یک درخواست این‌ها را می‌چسباند: - Setting - **همهٔ کاربران فعال** (`GetUserQuery` → کل `AppUsers`) - **همهٔ Positionها** با Users/Roles/Project - همهٔ Projectها و JobTitleها سپس فرانت با این دیتای عظیم می‌فهمد «سمت‌های هدیه خدام کدام‌اند». این دقیقاً همان چیزی است که باید در بک‌اند باشد. ### ۳) بقیهٔ فراخوانی‌ها `/dashboard` نسبتاً بهتر است (با `userId` فیلتر می‌کند) ولی باز هم خیلی داده را برای شمارش به حافظه می‌آورد. چت و نوتیفیکیشن هم همزمان با خانه لود می‌شوند؛ لازم نیست. ### چرا خطرناک است؟ - منطق کسب‌وکار (دسترسی، سمت، منو، گردش‌کار) روی کلاینت → باگ یا آپدیت ناقص اپ = سیستم خراب - ۱۰ هزار فرم × فیلدها = تأخیر، RAM، باتری، قطع شبکه - هر تغییر workflow باید هم بک‌اند هم Flutter را همزمان درست کند --- ## اصل اصلاح (یک جمله) **خانه = فقط «من کیستم + چه می‌توانم ببینم + چند تا عدد خلاصه».** جزئیات هر ماژول فقط وقتی کاربر روی همان دکمه زد. Flutter لازم نیست عمیق بلد باشی: بیشتر کار **طراحی API بک‌اند** است؛ Flutter فقط ترتیب فراخوانی را عوض می‌کند.
--- ## پیشنهاد ۱ — Bootstrap / Me (پیشنهاد نهایی من) فاز اول کم‌ریسک، بیشترین سود برای UX و امنیت منطق. ```mermaid flowchart TB subgraph Now["الان - بد"] L1[Login] --> F1[Sync/Form کل فرم‌ها] L1 --> S1[Sync/Setting کل یوزر+سمت] L1 --> D1[Dashboard] L1 --> C1[Chat + Notif] F1 --> Cache1[(کش عظیم روی موبایل)] S1 --> Logic1[منطق سمت/منو در Flutter] end subgraph Better["پیشنهاد ۱"] L2[Login فقط Token] --> B[GET /me/bootstrap] B --> P[پروفایل + سمت‌های من] B --> M[منوی مجاز خانه] B --> N[شمارنده‌های خلاصه] Tap[کلیک فرم] --> Cat[GET /forms/catalog] Tap2[باز کردن یک فرم] --> Det[GET /forms/{id}] Tap3[نامه / تسک / چت] --> Mod[API همان ماژول] end ``` **APIهای جدید پیشنهادی:** | API | نقش | |-----|-----| | `GET /me/bootstrap` | پروفایل، لیست سمت‌های **همین کاربر**، فلگ منو، badgeها | | `GET /me/positions` | فقط سمت‌های کاربر (دیگر کل org نیست) | | `GET /forms/catalog?type=&positionId=` | لیست سبک (id, name, type, category) بدون فیلد | | `GET /forms/{id}` | جزئیات + فیلدها وقتی لازم شد | | نگه‌داشتن `/dashboard` ولی فقط aggregate در SQL | نه کشیدن کل FormData | **مزیت:** سریع، منطق سمت بک‌اند، Flutter کم‌تغییر. **عیب:** هنوز باید مسیرهای قدیمی را تا مهاجرت نگه دارید (compat). --- ## پیشنهاد ۲ — BFF + Lazy Module (قوی‌تر برای مقیاس) اگر می‌خواهید خانه واقعاً سبک شود و هر دکمه قرارداد جدا داشته باشد. ```mermaid flowchart LR App[Flutter] -->|1. کوتاه| Boot[BFF Home] Boot --> HomeUI[خانه فوری] App -->|2. تپ فرم| FormsAPI[Forms Module API] App -->|3. تپ نامه| LetterAPI[Secretariat API] App -->|4. تپ تسک| TaskAPI[Task API] App -->|5. تپ پیام| ChatAPI[Chat API] FormsAPI --> Rules[قوانین دسترسی در بک‌اند] LetterAPI --> Rules ``` **قانون طلایی:** هیچ ماژولی در استارت اپ صدا زده نشود مگر برای badge ضروری (مثلاً فقط `unreadCount`). **مزیت:** جداسازی خطر؛ خرابی نامه ≠ خرابی فرم. **عیب:** نیاز به هماهنگی بیشتر با تیم Flutter و نسخهٔ اپ. --- ## پیشنهاد ۳ — Sync افزایشی + کش محلی (بلندمدت / آفلاین) اگر اپ باید آفلاین یا نیمه‌آفلاین کار کند. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant App as Flutter participant API as Customer API participant DB as SQL App->>API: GET /sync/manifest?since=etag API->>DB: فقط تغییرها API-->>App: delta فرم‌ها / سمت‌ها App->>App: SQLite محلی به‌روز Note over App: خانه از کش محلی
منطق دسترسی باز هم از سرور تأیید می‌شود ``` **مزیت:** سرعت بعدی عالی، ترافیک کم. **عیب:** پیچیده‌ترین؛ اگر منطق دسترسی را هنوز در فرانت بگذارید، همان فاجعه می‌ماند — فقط سریع‌تر می‌شود! ---

BFF عوض می‌شود: از `/home` به `/session/bootstrap` به‌جای endpoint مخصوص «خانه»، یک endpoint **نشست (Session)** داریم که **هیچ UI خانه‌ای نمی‌سازد**: ### `GET /session/bootstrap` (یا `/app/bootstrap`) **فقط این‌ها — سبک:** ```json { "profile": { "id": 12, "fullName": "هدیه خدام", "avatarUrl": "..." }, "positions": [ { "id": 5, "title": "مدیر شرکت لای", "isDefault": true }, { "id": 18, "title": "[توسعه عمران] مدیر HSE", "isDefault": false } ], "selectedPositionId": 5, "badges": { "notificationsUnread": 3, "messagesUnread": 12 }, "navigation": { "defaultModule": "forms", "chatEnabled": true, "secretariatEnabled": true } } ``` **عمداً نیست:** - آمار داشبورد (۱۱۲ فرم، ۲ آنومالی، …) - منوی آیکون‌های خانه - لیست فرم‌ها (آن در API خودش است) --- ## صفحهٔ پیش‌فرض = Forms Module ### اولین API که UI واقعی را پر می‌کند `GET /modules/forms/menu?positionId=5` خروجی: همان لیست سبک فرم‌های مجاز برای **سمت انتخاب‌شده**. این **همان صفحه‌ای است که کاربر می‌بیند** — مثل الان ولی: - بدون کش `Sync/Form` روی دستگاه - بدون منطق position در Flutter - فقط فرم‌های مجاز این سمت ### وقتی یک فرم باز شد `GET /modules/forms/{id}?positionId=5` → template + fields --- ## معماری کل — با Forms به‌عنوان مرکز ```mermaid flowchart TB subgraph Entry["ورود به اپ"] Login[Login → Token] Boot[GET /session/bootstrap] FormsMenu[GET /modules/forms/menu] Login --> Boot Login --> FormsMenu Boot --> FormsUI[صفحه فرم‌ها — پیش‌فرض] FormsMenu --> FormsUI end subgraph Nav["ناوبری پایین — بدون تب خانه"] TabForms[فرم‌ها ← پیش‌فرض / فعال] TabChat[پیام‌رسان] TabSettings[تنظیمات] end FormsUI --> TabForms TabChat -->|lazy| ChatAPI[GET /chat/conversations] TabSettings -->|lazy| SettingsAPI[تنظیمات + آمار اختیاری] ``` **تب «خانه» حذف می‌شود.** اولین تب = **فرم‌ها** (یا فقط همین یک صفحهٔ root بدون تب خانه). --- ## «آمارها» و آیکون‌های قبلی Home کجا می‌روند؟ چون Home نداریم، باید تصمیم بگیری — پیشنهاد من: | قبلاً روی Home | جای جدید | |----------------|----------| | آمار (۱۱۲ فرم، تسک، …) | **تنظیمات** → بخش «آمار» یا **lazy** با `GET /modules/stats/summary` | | اتوماسیون نامه‌نگاری | تب/منوی جدا — فقط وقتی کاربر رفت `GET /modules/secretariat/...` | | چک‌لیست / فرایند / … | زیرمجموعه **همان صفحه فرم‌ها** با فیلتر `type` | | position picker | **بالای صفحه فرم‌ها** (همان جایی که الان positionها را نشان می‌دهی) | **صفحه فرم‌ها می‌تواند چند نوع داشته باشد** (Form, CheckList, Process, …) با **segment/tab داخلی** یا فیلتر — نه صفحه Home جدا. مثال: ``` GET /modules/forms/menu?positionId=5&type=Form GET /modules/forms/menu?positionId=5&type=CheckList ``` کاربر segment را عوض کند → همان صفحه، API با `type` متفاوت. --- ## مقایسه تعداد API — Login path | حالت | بعد از Login | |------|----------------| | **قدیم** | ۷+ API سنگین | | **روش ۲ + Home** | 1 bootstrap + lazy | | **روش ۲ + Forms پیش‌فرض** | **2 API سبک موازی** (`bootstrap` + `forms/menu`) ≈ همان سرعت، UX بهتر برای شما | دو درخواست موازی معمولاً از **یک Sync/Form** سبک‌تر است. --- ## ساختار endpoint — به‌روز | Endpoint | نقش | |----------|-----| | `POST /Auth/Login` | Token | | `GET /session/bootstrap` | profile, positions, badges, flags — **بدون UI Home** | | `GET /modules/forms/menu` | **صفحه پیش‌فرض** | | `GET /modules/forms/{id}` | باز کردن یک فرم | | `GET /modules/stats/summary` | **اختیاری / lazy** — جای آمار Home | | `GET /chat/conversations` | فقط تب پیام | | `GET /modules/secretariat/...` | فقط بخش نامه | | ~~`GET /home`~~ | **حذف** | | ~~`Sync/Form` در login~~ | **حذف از مسیر ورود** | --- ## Dashboard فعلی (`GET /dashboard`) چه می‌شود؟ - دیگر موقع login صدا زده **نمی‌شود** - یا **ادغام** در `GET /modules/stats/summary` (فقط وقتی کاربر آمار خواست) - یا **حذف از mobile** و فقط در Blazor/گزارش بماند این با «Forms پیش‌فرض» سازگار است — آمار blocking نیست. --- ## Flutter — چک‌لیست برای تیم موبایل 1. `initialRoute` = **FormsScreen** (نه Home) 2. بعد login: `bootstrap` + `forms/menu` **موازی** 3. تب bottom nav: **خانه حذف**؛ اولین item = فرم‌ها (یا root همان Forms) 4. position picker روی **FormsScreen** 5. `Sync/Form` و `Sync/Setting` از startup حذف 6. آمار قبلی Home → Settings یا drawer «آمار» --- ## فاز پیاده‌سازی — با این تغییر | فاز | کار | |-----|-----| | **۱** | `IUserContextService` + `GET /session/bootstrap` | | **۲** | `GET /modules/forms/menu` + `GET /modules/forms/{id}` — **اولویت بالا** چون default screen است | | **۳** | segment/type برای CheckList, Process, … روی همان صفحه | | **۴** | stats/secretariat/chat — lazy | | **۵** | deprecate `Sync/Form` + حذف Home از Flutter | --- ## جمع‌بندی - **Home UI حذف** → کاربر مستقیم **صفحه فرم‌ها** را می‌بیند - **BFF** دیگر «Home» نیست → **`/session/bootstrap`** (context سبک) - **اولین محتوای واقعی** = **`/modules/forms/menu`** - منطق position و دسترسی **همچنان ۱۰۰٪ بک‌اند** - آمار و نامه‌نگاری **lazy** — نه blocking ورود اگر موافقی، قدم بعد در Agent mode می‌تواند **`session/bootstrap` + `modules/forms/menu`** باشد — بدون هیچ endpoint یا صفحهٔ Home.

# Mobile Forms Hub + Lazy Modules — Phased Implementation Guide

> **Status:** Planning only — do **not** implement until the product owner explicitly says to start a phase.  
> **Audience:** Coding agent (and humans) working on Elay Customer API.  
> **Architecture choice:** Option 2 — BFF/Session bootstrap + Lazy Module APIs.  
> **Default screen:** Forms hub (workflow tiles) — **not** Home dashboard, **not** form list.  
> **Language of this doc:** English (implementation precision). Product discussion with owner may be Persian.

---

## 0. How to use this document

### Rules for the agent

1. **Never implement more than one phase in a single session** unless the owner explicitly asks for multiple phases.
2. Before coding a phase, re-read:
   - This file (the target phase section)
   - `FORMS_SYSTEM.md` (root)
   - `.cursor/rules/forms-system.mdc`
   - Relevant existing code via **codebase-memory MCP** (`search_graph` / `get_code_snippet`) — not Grep for `.cs`
3. For every new Command/Query: follow **scenario-contract** (`.cursor/skills/scenario-contract`) and wait for `approved` / `تایید شد` before scaffolding.
4. End each phase with **verify-feature** (build + API smoke for touched endpoints).
5. Do **not** touch: `Program.cs`, `Migrations/**`, `Share/SharedKernel.Domain/Abstractions/**`.
6. Keep old mobile endpoints alive until Phase 6 (compat). Do not break current Flutter until a coordinated app release.

### Phase gate checklist (copy into chat before starting a phase)

```
Phase: N — <name>
Owner said start: YES / NO
Sections I will read: [...]
Files I will CREATE: [...]
Files I will MODIFY: [...]
Assumptions: [...]
Scenario-contract required: YES / NO
Out of scope this phase: [...]
```

### Related docs

| Doc | Why |
|-----|-----|
| `FORMS_SYSTEM.md` | Form lifecycle, sync, PermitNew vs FormRole |
| `Customer/Api/Docs/Auth.md` | Login / token |
| `Customer/Api/Docs/FormData.md` | Submissions (`FormData`) |
| `Share/SharedKernel.Domain/Enums/FormType.cs` | Canonical type enum |

---

## 1. Problem statement (why we are doing this)

### Current mobile login path (anti-pattern)

After login, Flutter typically calls many heavy APIs at once, for example:

- `GET /Sync/Form` — **all** active templates + fields (no per-user filter in handler)
- `GET /Sync/Setting` — settings + **all users** + **all positions** + projects + job titles
- `GET /dashboard`
- `GET /Notification`
- `GET /chat/conversations` (+ often a conversation detail)
- `GET /chat/profile`

Business logic (which positions belong to the user, which tiles to show) is inferred **on the client** from bulk data. That is slow, fragile, and dangerous for workflow/secretariat regressions.

### Evidence in current backend (do not “fix” casually)

| Area | Behavior today |
|------|----------------|
| `SyncFormEndPoint` → `FormAllQueryHandler` | Loads all active `FormTemplates` + fields; `ICurrentUserService` unused for filtering |
| `SyncSettingEndPoint` | Fan-in: Setting + `GetUserQuery` (all active users) + `GetAllPositionsQuery` (full org) + projects + jobs |
| `GetUserQueryHandler` | Loads **all** `AppUsers`, then moves current user to index 0 |
| `DashboardQueryHandler` | Filters by user, but loads large sets into memory for counts |
| Task | Separate domain (`TaskItems`) — must **not** live under Forms hub |

---

## 2. Target product UX (locked decisions)

### Default entry after login

1. User logs in → tokens only from `/Auth/Login`.
2. App loads a **light session bootstrap** (profile, positions, badges, which tiles are visible).
3. User lands on tab **فرم‌ها (Forms)** = **workflow hub** with **four buttons only** — **no form list yet**, no Home dashboard, no stats block, no secretariat block.

### Forms hub tiles (workflow buttons)

| UI label (Persian) | Backend type | `FormType` |
|--------------------|--------------|------------|
| فرم | Form | `Form = 1` |
| چک لیست | CheckList | `CheckList = 2` |
| فرآیند | Process | `PermitNew = 6` |
| مسیر اقدام | Action path | `Emergency = 4` |

**Not on Forms hub:**

| Item | Placement |
|------|-----------|
| تسک (Task) | **Separate bottom-nav tab** — different infrastructure (`TaskItems`) |
| خانه (Home) | **Removed** as default / primary screen |
| اتوماسیون نامه‌نگاری | Lazy — only when user opens that module |
| آمارها | Lazy — e.g. under Settings or a stats module API |

### On tile click

- Call **one type-scoped API** (same route shape, different `type` query).
- Only then show the **list** for that type (filtered by access + selected position).
- Opening one item → detail API for that template/id (fields included only here).

### Bottom navigation (target)

| Tab | Role |
|-----|------|
| فرم‌ها | Default — Forms hub (4 tiles) |
| تسک | Separate module |
| پیام‌رسان | Chat — lazy on tab open |
| تنظیمات | Settings / optional stats — lazy |

---

## 3. Target architecture (Option 2 adapted)

```text
Login (token only)
        │
        ▼
GET /session/bootstrap     ← light context for hub chrome (NOT Home UI)
        │
        ▼
[Forms hub UI: 4 tiles]    ← default screen
        │
        ├── click Form        → GET /modules/forms/menu?type=Form&positionId=
        ├── click CheckList   → GET /modules/forms/menu?type=CheckList&positionId=
        ├── click PermitNew   → GET /modules/forms/menu?type=PermitNew&positionId=
        └── click Emergency   → GET /modules/forms/menu?type=Emergency&positionId=
                │
                └── open item → GET /modules/forms/{id}?positionId=

[Task tab]  → GET /modules/tasks/...   (separate stack; not FormType)
[Chat tab]  → existing /chat/*         (not on login)
[Stats]     → GET /modules/stats/...   (optional / lazy)
```

### Shared application services (core of the refactor)

| Service | Responsibility |
|---------|----------------|
| `IUserContextService` | Current user profile; **my** positions; default/selected position; role ids for those positions |
| `IFormAccessService` | Which form template ids the user/position may see (`FormRole`, `FormTemplatePositions`, PermitNew rules) |
| `IHomeMenuService` / rename to `IWorkflowTileService` | Which of the 4 tiles are `visible` for this user/position (no Task here) |

> Prefer naming **`IWorkflowTileService`** over Home* — product has no Home screen.

### Hard rules

1. **No bulk `Sync/Form` on login** for the new app path.
2. **No “all users / all positions”** on login — positions = **mine only**.
3. **Access decisions live on the server** — Flutter must not filter org graphs to discover positions.
4. **Task ≠ FormType** — never put Task inside forms menu APIs.
5. **PermitStep** is not a hub tile; it appears under process/`PermitNew` flows.
6. **Anomaly** has no hub tile in v1 unless product asks later.
7. Old endpoints stay until Phase 6 deprecation.

---

## 4. Proposed API contracts (draft — refine per phase)

### 4.1 `GET /session/bootstrap`

**Auth:** required  
**Purpose:** Chrome for Forms hub + nav flags. **Not** a dashboard. **Not** form lists.

```json
{
  "profile": {
    "id": 0,
    "fullName": "string",
    "avatarUrl": "string|null"
  },
  "positions": [
    { "id": 0, "title": "string", "isDefault": true }
  ],
  "selectedPositionId": 0,
  "badges": {
    "notificationsUnread": 0,
    "messagesUnread": 0,
    "tasksPending": 0
  },
  "workflowTiles": [
    { "type": "Form", "title": "فرم", "visible": true, "badge": 0 },
    { "type": "CheckList", "title": "چک لیست", "visible": true, "badge": 0 },
    { "type": "PermitNew", "title": "فرآیند", "visible": true, "badge": 0 },
    { "type": "Emergency", "title": "مسیر اقدام", "visible": true, "badge": 0 }
  ],
  "navigation": {
    "defaultTab": "forms",
    "tasksTabEnabled": true,
    "chatTabEnabled": true,
    "settingsTabEnabled": true
  },
  "settingsLite": {
    "minAppVersion": "string|null"
  }
}
```

**Must not include:** form templates, fields, full user directory, full org chart, chat conversations, dashboard charts.

### 4.2 `GET /modules/forms/menu`

**Query:**

| Param | Required | Notes |
|-------|----------|-------|
| `type` | yes | `Form` \| `CheckList` \| `PermitNew` \| `Emergency` (reject others for hub path) |
| `positionId` | yes | Must be one of the user’s positions (403/400 if not) |
| `pageIndex` / `pageSize` | optional | Prefer pagination from day one if lists can be large |

**Response item (light):** id, name, type, categoryName, order (`NO`), optional code — **no fields**.

### 4.3 `GET /modules/forms/{id}`

**Query:** `positionId` (validate access)  
**Response:** template detail + **active fields** (what Flutter used to get from `Sync/Form` for one form).

### 4.4 Task (separate — later phase)

Do not design Task inside Forms menu. Reuse/extend existing Task endpoints under `/modules/tasks/...` naming in a dedicated phase.

### 4.5 Stats (optional)

`GET /modules/stats/summary?positionId=` — replaces login-time `/dashboard` for mobile. Lazy only.

---

## 5. Phased implementation plan

Each phase has: **Goal**, **In scope**, **Out of scope**, **Files (expected)**, **Acceptance criteria**, **Tests**, **Stop condition**.

---

### Phase 0 — Baseline & contract freeze

**Goal:** Measure current pain; freeze product/API decisions; no production behavior change.

**In scope**

- Document measured response sizes/times for `/Sync/Form`, `/Sync/Setting`, `/dashboard` (Postman; note env).
- Confirm tile mapping (this doc Section 2) with owner — already decided; re-confirm if app labels drift.
- List Flutter login call graph (owner/mobile team) for deprecation planning.
- Decide pagination defaults for menu (`PageSize`).

**Out of scope**

- Any new endpoint or handler code.

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Baseline numbers recorded in this doc appendix or a linked note.
- [ ] Owner confirms: start Phase 1 when ready.

**Stop:** Wait for owner: “Start Phase 1”.

---

### Phase 1 — User context + session bootstrap

**Goal:** Server owns “who am I” and “my positions”; provide `/session/bootstrap` for Forms hub chrome.

**In scope**

- `IUserContextService` (+ Infrastructure implementation).
  - Resolve user id from `ICurrentUserService`.
  - Load **only positions linked to this user** (junction `Users` + legacy `AppUser.OrgPositionId` if still needed — mirror logic already present in `GetAllPositionsQueryHandler` but **filtered**).
  - Build display titles (including `[ParentProject] Title` pattern if applicable).
- `IWorkflowTileService` — for each of the 4 types, set `visible` (v1 may set all true if access unknown; prefer real access if cheap).
- DTOs: `SessionBootstrapDto`, position/tile/nav DTOs.
- CQRS: `GetSessionBootstrapQuery` + Handler + Validator (if needed).
- Carter: `SessionBootstrapEndPoint` → `GET /session/bootstrap`.
- Register services in Infrastructure DI (not `Program.cs` if registration lives in existing `DependencyInjection` extensions).

**Out of scope**

- Forms menu / detail.
- Changing `Sync/*` behavior.
- Task / Chat / Dashboard changes.
- Flutter changes (document contract for mobile only).

**Scenario-contract:** **YES** (new Query).

**Expected files (illustrative — verify paths before create)**

```
Customer/Core/Application/Services/Interface/IUserContextService.cs
Customer/Core/Application/Services/Interface/IWorkflowTileService.cs
Customer/Infrastructure/Services/UserContextService.cs
Customer/Infrastructure/Services/WorkflowTileService.cs
Customer/Core/Application/DTOs/Session/SessionBootstrapDto.cs
Customer/Core/Application/Features/App/Session/Queries/GetSessionBootstrap/...
Customer/Api/EndPoint/Session/SessionBootstrapEndPoint.cs
```

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Authenticated call returns only current user’s positions (not full org).
- [ ] `workflowTiles` has exactly the four types; **no Task tile**.
- [ ] Response is small (target: tens of KB, not MB).
- [ ] Build passes; Postman smoke with ChatTest user.

**Stop:** Owner reviews sample JSON; then “Start Phase 2”.

---

### Phase 2 — Form access service + forms menu by type

**Goal:** Clicking a hub tile loads a **type-scoped light list** with server-side access control.

**In scope**

- `IFormAccessService`
  - Inputs: `userId`, `positionId`, `FormType`.
  - Consider: `FormRole`, `FormTemplatePositions`, active flags.
  - PermitNew: follow `FORMS_SYSTEM.md` (workflow ≠ FormRole only). Document assumptions; escalate if unclear.
- `GetFormMenuQuery` → `GET /modules/forms/menu?type=&positionId=`
- Validate `type` ∈ { Form, CheckList, PermitNew, Emergency }.
- Validate `positionId` belongs to current user (reuse `IUserContextService`).
- Light DTO list; pagination preferred.
- Endpoint module under `Api/EndPoint/Modules/Forms/`.

**Out of scope**

- Form detail/fields.
- Removing or changing `Sync/Form`.
- Anomaly menu unless owner requests.

**Scenario-contract:** **YES**.

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Same user, two positions → menus can differ when access differs.
- [ ] Wrong `positionId` → failure (not empty silent leak of other data).
- [ ] Invalid hub `type` (e.g. Task) → validation error.
- [ ] No field payloads in menu response.
- [ ] Build + Postman for all 4 types.

**Stop:** Owner “Start Phase 3”.

---

### Phase 3 — Form template detail (replaces per-form Sync/Form usage)

**Goal:** Opening one form loads full template+fields for that id only, with access check.

**In scope**

- `GetFormTemplateDetailQuery` → `GET /modules/forms/{id}?positionId=`
- Reuse mapping patterns from `FormAllQueryHandler` / existing DTOs where safe — but **scoped to one form**.
- Deny if not accessible for user/position.
- Active fields only.

**Out of scope**

- FormData submit changes.
- Sync pipeline Admin→Customer.

**Scenario-contract:** **YES**.

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Detail size ≪ full `Sync/Form`.
- [ ] Unauthorized form id → NotFound or AccessForbidden (pick one and document).
- [ ] Build + Postman.

**Stop:** Owner “Start Phase 4”.

---

### Phase 4 — Stats lazy API (optional dashboard replacement)

**Goal:** Move `/dashboard` off the login path; provide on-demand stats.

**In scope**

- `GetStatsSummaryQuery` → `GET /modules/stats/summary?positionId=` (or without position if stats are user-scoped like today).
- Prefer SQL aggregates / counts over loading all `FormData` into memory (improve `DashboardQueryHandler` patterns).
- Keep old `/dashboard` working for old app.

**Out of scope**

- UI Home recreation.

**Scenario-contract:** **YES** if new query.

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Numbers roughly consistent with old dashboard for a test user (document known diffs).
- [ ] Not called from new login path (mobile contract note).

**Stop:** Owner may skip this phase; then jump to Phase 5.

---

### Phase 5 — Task tab contract (separate module)

**Goal:** Clarify Task as first-class tab API path; do not mix with Forms.

**In scope**

- Inventory existing Task endpoints (codebase-memory).
- Either document “use existing Task APIs from Task tab” **or** add thin `GET /modules/tasks/summary` for badge/list entry — **only if needed**.
- Ensure `session/bootstrap` exposes `tasksTabEnabled` + optional `tasksPending` badge only.

**Out of scope**

- Rewriting Task domain.
- Putting Task under `/modules/forms/*`.

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Written contract for Flutter: Task tab call sequence.
- [ ] No Task type on forms menu.

**Stop:** Owner “Start Phase 6”.

---

### Phase 6 — Compat, deprecation, Flutter coordination

**Goal:** New app uses new path; old endpoints deprecated safely.

**In scope**

- Written deprecation policy for:
  - `GET /Sync/Form` (mobile bootstrap)
  - `GET /Sync/Setting` bulk users/positions (mobile bootstrap)
  - Login-time chat/notification fan-out
- Optional: response header / log warning when deprecated endpoints are hit.
- Feature flag or app-version gate if available in settings.
- **Do not delete** endpoints until mobile adoption criteria met (define % or date with owner).

**Out of scope**

- Hard delete without owner approval.

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Flutter checklist delivered (Persian/English as needed).
- [ ] Old app still works.
- [ ] New app login path documented as: Login → bootstrap → Forms hub → tile click → menu → detail.

**Stop:** Release coordination; only then consider Phase 7.

---

### Phase 7 — Hardening (after new app ships)

**Goal:** Performance and safety once traffic moves.

**In scope (pick with owner)**

- Indexes / query tuning for access checks.
- Stricter tile visibility from real permissions.
- Incremental sync **only if** offline requirements appear (do not start without owner).
- Eventually disable or restrict deprecated sync endpoints for mobile clients.

**Out of scope**

- Big-bang rewrite of Admin sync.

---

## 6. Flutter coordination checklist (give to mobile team)

New login path:

1. `POST /Auth/Login` → store tokens.
2. `GET /session/bootstrap` → render Forms hub tiles + position picker + badges.
3. **Do not** call `Sync/Form`, `Sync/Setting`, `/dashboard`, chat list, or notification list on login.
4. On tile click → `GET /modules/forms/menu?type=...&positionId=...`.
5. On item open → `GET /modules/forms/{id}?positionId=...`.
6. Task tab → Task APIs only.
7. Chat / notifications → on tab/screen open (badge counts may come from bootstrap only).

---

## 7. Risk register

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Wrong access rules for PermitNew | Read `FORMS_SYSTEM.md`; scenario-contract; compare with existing permit handlers |
| Breaking old Flutter | Compat until Phase 6; no deletion early |
| Position title format mismatch | Copy existing title logic from `GetAllPositionsQueryHandler` |
| Agent implements multiple phases at once | This doc + owner gate phrases |
| Logic leaks back to Flutter | Code review: no “download all then filter” endpoints for new path |
| Task mixed into forms | Explicit Phase 5; validation reject Task on forms menu |

---

## 8. Sensitive domains reminder

Forms / Permit / OrgPosition / Notification touch sensitive areas. For new Commands/Queries:

1. Run **scenario-contract**.
2. Wait for **approved** / **تایید شد**.
3. Then scaffold / implement **one phase**.
4. **verify-feature**.

---

## 9. Owner control phrases

| Owner says | Agent does |
|------------|------------|
| *(nothing / “don’t start coding”)* | Only docs / answers |
| `Start Phase 0` | Baseline only |
| `Start Phase 1` | Bootstrap stack only |
| `Start Phase 2` | Menu by type only |
| … | … |
| `Stop` / `pause` | No further implementation |

---

## 10. Appendix A — Current endpoints to leave alone until Phase 6

| Endpoint | Role today | New path replacement |
|----------|------------|----------------------|
| `GET /Sync/Form` | Bulk templates+fields | `/modules/forms/menu` + `/modules/forms/{id}` |
| `GET /Sync/Setting` | Settings+all users+all positions | `/session/bootstrap` (+ search endpoints when picking people) |
| `GET /dashboard` | Stats on login | `/modules/stats/summary` (lazy) |
| `GET /Notification` | List | Keep; call when needed |
| `GET /chat/*` | Chat | Keep; call on chat tab |
| `GET /Message/Positions` | Paginated position search | Keep for secretariat pickers |

---

## 11. Appendix B — Architecture diagram (reference)

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Login[POST /Auth/Login] --> Boot[GET /session/bootstrap]
  Boot --> Hub[Forms hub — 4 tiles]

  Hub -->|type=Form| M1[GET /modules/forms/menu]
  Hub -->|type=CheckList| M2[GET /modules/forms/menu]
  Hub -->|type=PermitNew| M3[GET /modules/forms/menu]
  Hub -->|type=Emergency| M4[GET /modules/forms/menu]

  M1 & M2 & M3 & M4 --> Det[GET /modules/forms/id]

  TabTask[Task tab] --> Tasks[Task module APIs]
  TabChat[Chat tab] --> Chat[Existing chat APIs]
  TabSet[Settings] --> Stats[Optional stats API]
```

---

## 12. Document history

| Date | Change |
|------|--------|
| 2026-07-22 | Initial phased guide: Forms hub default, Emergency = مسیر اقدام, Task separate tab, no code yet |

---

**End of guide.** Wait for explicit phase start from the owner before any implementation.

# Mobile Forms Hub API Documentation

> **Status:** Contract / planned APIs for the Forms-hub + Lazy Modules refactor.  
> **Implementation guide:** `Customer/Api/Docs/Mobile-FormsHub-LazyModules-Implementation-Guide.md`  
> **Do not implement** until the product owner starts a phase in that guide.  
> **Template:** Same structure as `Customer/Api/Docs/Auth.md` (Route, Command/Query, Input, Validator, Handler, Return DTO, Errors, Pagination, Notes).

---

## Event Index (all APIs in this feature)

| # | Event (API) | Method | URL | Status | Phase | Role |
|---|-------------|--------|-----|--------|-------|------|
| 1 | Login | `POST` | `/Auth/Login` | EXISTING | — | Token only; entry to app |
| 2 | Session Bootstrap | `GET` | `/session/bootstrap` | **PLANNED** | 1 | Forms hub chrome: profile, my positions, tiles, badges |
| 3 | Forms Menu By Type | `GET` | `/modules/forms/menu` | **PLANNED** | 2 | List after tile click (`type` + `positionId`) |
| 4 | Form Template Detail | `GET` | `/modules/forms/{id}` | **PLANNED** | 3 | One template + fields when user opens an item |
| 5 | Stats Summary | `GET` | `/modules/stats/summary` | **PLANNED** (optional) | 4 | Lazy stats; replaces login-time `/dashboard` |
| 6 | Task tab APIs | — | Task module (existing / thin wrapper) | EXISTING + Phase 5 | 5 | Separate bottom-nav tab — **not** FormType |
| 7 | FormData list (by type) | `GET` | `/FormData/{type}` | EXISTING | — | Submissions list (after menu/detail flow) |
| 8 | FormData get by id | `GET` | `/FormData/{id}` | EXISTING | — | One submission |
| 9 | FormData create/update | `POST` | `/FormData` | EXISTING | — | Submit / save filled form |

### Login-path legacy events (must NOT run on new-app startup)

| # | Event (API) | Method | URL | Status | Notes |
|---|-------------|--------|-----|--------|-------|
| L1 | Sync Form (bulk) | `GET` | `/Sync/Form` | EXISTING — deprecate from login (Phase 6) | All templates + fields |
| L2 | Sync Setting (bulk) | `GET` | `/Sync/Setting` | EXISTING — deprecate from login (Phase 6) | Settings + all users + all positions + … |
| L3 | Dashboard | `GET` | `/dashboard` | EXISTING — lazy via #5 | Stats on login today |
| L4 | Notifications page | `GET` | `/Notification` | EXISTING — lazy | Call when user opens notifications |

## New-app event sequence (happy path)

```text
1. POST /Auth/Login
2. GET  /session/bootstrap          → show Forms hub (4 tiles)
3. GET  /modules/forms/menu?type=X&positionId=Y   → list for tile X
4. GET  /modules/forms/{id}?positionId=Y          → open template
… optional FormData events 7–9 when filling/submitting
… Task tab → Event group 6
… Stats → Event 5
… Chat / Notification → Legacy D–G when opened
```

# Flutter Migration Guide — Mobile Forms Hub + Lazy Modules

> **Audience:** Frontend engineer + Cursor agent working on the **Flutter** app.  
> **Language:** English (for agent precision). Product owner may discuss in Persian.  
> **Sources of truth (read both before any code):**
>
> 1. `Customer/Api/Docs/Mobile-FormsHub-LazyModules-Implementation-Guide.md` — backend phases  
> 2. `Customer/Api/Docs/MobileFormsHub.md` — API event catalog (every route)  
>
> **Backend APIs marked PLANNED are not live until backend owner starts each phase.**  
> Frontend must not switch production traffic to missing endpoints.

---

## 0. CRITICAL WARNING — read this twice

This refactor changes **how the app loads APIs after login**. It does **not** intentionally change **business rules** of forms, permits, approvals, secretariat, or tasks.

| If you get this wrong… | Result |
|------------------------|--------|
| Wrong form type mapping | Wrong lists / empty screens / broken workflows |
| Client-side position filtering kept while also trusting bootstrap | Inconsistent access / security bugs |
| Removing `Sync/Form` before detail API works | **All forms explode** — cannot open templates |
| Mixing Task into Forms hub | Wrong module, wrong data |
| Changing FormData submit payload “while cleaning” | Submissions fail silently or corrupt data |
| Big-bang rewrite of Home + Sync + Form open in one PR | Impossible to bisect; high blast radius |

### Absolute rules for human + Cursor agent

1. **Proceed with extreme caution.** Prefer small PRs. One migration step per PR when possible.
2. **Do not change business logic** (who can approve, workflow steps, field validation rules, permit rules) unless the product owner explicitly asks. This work is **API architecture + loading timing + UI shell**.
3. **Touch business logic only when unavoidable** (e.g. you discover the app cannot open a form without a client-side rule that backend now owns). Then **stop**, document the rule, ask backend/product — do not invent a new rule.
4. **Keep a rollback path** until the new path is proven (feature flag or compile-time switch).
5. **Never delete old Sync calls** until the new path fully covers: hub → menu by type → open detail → existing FormData submit still works.
6. **Do not “optimize” FormData POST/GET** in this refactor unless required for compile.
7. Owner of Flutter codebase may not know every corner — **discover with search before editing**; do not guess file names.

### Cursor agent preamble (paste at the start of every Flutter chat)

```text
You are migrating Elay Flutter to Forms-hub + Lazy Modules.
Read and obey:
- Customer/Api/Docs/MobileFormsHub-Flutter-Migration-Guide.md (this guide)
- Customer/Api/Docs/MobileFormsHub.md (API events)
- Customer/Api/Docs/Mobile-FormsHub-LazyModules-Implementation-Guide.md (phases)

HARD RULES:
1) Do NOT change form/permit/task BUSINESS rules — only API call timing, caching, and navigation shell.
2) Do NOT remove Sync/Form or Sync/Setting until the replacement path works end-to-end behind a feature flag.
3) One migration step at a time. Stop after each step for human verification.
4) Task is a SEPARATE tab — not a Forms hub tile. Emergency = مسیر اقدام. PermitNew = فرآیند.
5) Positions come ONLY from GET /session/bootstrap — never filter full org lists on client to discover "my" positions.
6) If you must touch business logic, STOP and ask — do not invent rules.
```

---

## 1. Goal in one paragraph

**Today:** After login, Flutter fires many heavy APIs (`Sync/Form`, `Sync/Setting`, dashboard, chat, notifications), caches huge data, and decides positions/menus on the client.

**Target:** After login → **token** → **`GET /session/bootstrap`** → land on **Forms hub** (4 tiles only). Each tile click → **`GET /modules/forms/menu?type=…&positionId=…`**. Open one form → **`GET /modules/forms/{id}?positionId=…`**. Task / Chat / Stats / Notifications load **only when that UI opens**. FormData submit APIs stay as they are.

---

## 2. Locked product decisions (do not reinterpret)

### Default screen

- **Remove Home as default** (خانه is no longer the first screen).
- **Default tab = فرم‌ها** = **hub of 4 buttons**, **not** a form list.

### Four hub tiles only

| Persian UI | `type` query string | Backend `FormType` |
|------------|---------------------|--------------------|
| فرم | `Form` | `1` |
| چک لیست | `CheckList` | `2` |
| فرآیند | `PermitNew` | `6` |
| مسیر اقدام | `Emergency` | `4` |

### Explicit exclusions

| Item | Rule |
|------|------|
| تسک | **Separate bottom-nav tab** — different stack; **never** call forms menu with Task |
| آمارها | Lazy (Settings or stats screen) — not login |
| نامه‌نگاری | Lazy — not login |
| Chat / Notification lists | Lazy — not login |
| `Anomaly` | No hub tile in v1 |
| `PermitStep` | Not a hub tile (under process flow) |

---

## 3. Coordination with backend (do not skip)

| Backend phase | Frontend may start | Frontend must NOT yet |
|---------------|--------------------|------------------------|
| Phase 0 | Discovery + flag scaffolding only | Switch traffic |
| Phase 1 live (`/session/bootstrap`) | Wire bootstrap behind flag; keep Sync as fallback | Delete Sync/Setting |
| Phase 2 live (`/modules/forms/menu`) | Tile → menu behind flag | Delete Sync/Form |
| Phase 3 live (`/modules/forms/{id}`) | Open form via detail API behind flag | Delete Sync/Form cache dependency |
| Phase 4+ | Stats / Task polish | — |
| Phase 6 | Remove login Sync waterfall | — |

**Gate:** Before coding against a PLANNED URL, confirm with backend owner: “Is Phase N deployed on this environment?”

---

## 4. What must NOT change (business / domain)

Treat these as **sacred** unless owner says otherwise:

- Form fill / validation UX rules already approved by business
- Approve / reject / workflow step behavior
- PermitNew process behavior
- FormData request body shape and multipart file upload
- Task domain rules (only **when** Task APIs are called — timing changes only)
- Chat send/read behavior (only **when** chat opens)

**Allowed changes:**

- Which APIs run after login
- Where position list comes from
- Navigation: Home → Forms hub; Task as own tab
- Cache strategy (stop caching entire Sync/Form on startup)
- Feature flags / remote config for gradual rollout

---

## 5. Recommended rollout strategy (mandatory)

### Feature flag

Introduce a single flag, e.g. `useFormsHubLazyModules` (local or remote):

| Flag | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `false` (default initially) | **Exact old login waterfall** — production-safe |
| `true` | New path: bootstrap + hub + lazy modules |

### Rules

1. Ship flag `false` first (code exists but inactive).
2. Enable for internal testers only.
3. Compare: open Form / CheckList / PermitNew / Emergency / submit FormData / Task tab / Chat.
4. Only then widen rollout.
5. Keep old code path until Phase 6 deprecation sign-off.

**Do not** replace production login in one commit without the flag.

---

## 6. Discovery checklist (Flutter codebase — before edits)

Cursor/human must **find** (search) and list paths in the PR description — do not invent:

1. Login success handler / post-auth bootstrap
2. Calls to `/Sync/Form`, `/Sync/Setting`, `/dashboard`, `/Notification`, `/chat/conversations`, `/chat/profile`
3. Local cache / Hive / SQLite / shared prefs storing forms & settings
4. Logic that filters positions by `userId` / `UserIds.contains`
5. Home / dashboard widget and bottom navigation
6. Navigation to Form / CheckList / Process / Emergency / Task
7. Code that opens a form template (reads fields from Sync cache)
8. FormData submit client

**Output of discovery (required before Step A code):** a short map:

```text
Post-login entry: <file>
Sync/Form caller: <file>
Sync/Setting caller: <file>
Position filter logic: <file>
Home screen: <file>
Bottom nav: <file>
Open form template: <file>
FormData submit: <file>
```

If any item is “unknown”, **stop and search more** — do not guess.

---

## 7. Step-by-step migration (frontend)

Each step: **Goal → Do → Do not → Verify → Stop**.

---

### Step A — Discovery + feature flag only

**Goal:** Safe scaffolding; zero behavior change.

**Do**

1. Complete Section 6 discovery map.
2. Add `useFormsHubLazyModules` default `false`.
3. Document current post-login call list in a comment or short `MIGRATION_NOTES.md` in the Flutter repo (optional but recommended).

**Do not**

- Change navigation.
- Remove or reorder API calls.

**Verify**

- [ ] App behaves identically to before.
- [ ] Flag false on cold start.

**Stop** for human smoke test (login + open one form + submit if possible).

---

### Step B — Navigation shell only (still old APIs)

**Goal:** UX shell matches target **without** switching data loading yet.

**Do**

1. Make **Forms hub** (4 tiles) the default tab/screen when flag is true **or** even when false if you only change UI labels carefully — **prefer behind flag**.
2. Remove Task from the workflow row if it currently sits among Form/CheckList/Process/Emergency; put Task on bottom nav.
3. Keep Home content available under Settings or behind a temporary entry if product needs stats during transition — **do not delete stats UI**, only stop showing it as default when flag true.

**Do not**

- Remove Sync calls yet.
- Change how forms open.

**Verify**

- [ ] Flag false: old Home still works if still required.
- [ ] Flag true: land on 4-tile hub; Task is separate tab; tiles do not load lists yet (or still use old cache if needed — prefer placeholders until Step D).

**Stop** for UX review with product owner.

---

### Step C — Wire `GET /session/bootstrap` (flag true) — keep Sync fallback

**Depends on:** Backend Phase 1 live.

**Goal:** Positions + profile + tiles + badges from server; **no client org filtering**.

**Do**

1. After login (token saved), if flag true → `GET /session/bootstrap` with Bearer token.
2. Map response to app state:
   - profile display name / avatar
   - `positions` → position picker source of truth
   - `selectedPositionId`
   - `workflowTiles` → which of 4 buttons visible
   - badges for bell / chat / task tab
3. **Remove or bypass** client logic like “load all positions from Sync/Setting then `UserIds.contains(me)`” **when flag true**.
4. If bootstrap fails → show error; optionally fall back to old Sync path (recommended during rollout).

**Do not**

- Stop calling Sync/Form yet (still needed to open forms until Step E).
- Assume tile `visible=false` means “delete button code” without product confirmation.
- Call chat/notification **lists** here.

**Verify**

- [ ] User with 2 positions sees the same two titles as agreed with backend (e.g. هدیه خدام case).
- [ ] Changing selected position updates app state only (lists still old until Step D).
- [ ] Flag false still uses Sync/Setting for positions.

**Stop** for product + backend confirmation of position titles.

---

### Step D — Tile click → `GET /modules/forms/menu` (flag true)

**Depends on:** Backend Phase 2 live.

**Goal:** Each tile loads **only that type’s light list**.

**Do**

1. On tile tap, call:

```http
GET /modules/forms/menu?type={Form|CheckList|PermitNew|Emergency}&positionId={selected}
```

2. Pass **exact** type strings from Section 2.
3. Render list from menu DTO (id, name, …) — **no fields expected**.
4. Keep `positionId` in sync with picker; on position change while on a list screen → **re-fetch menu**.

**Do not**

- Call menu with `Task` or `Anomaly`.
- Use Sync/Form list for that tile when flag true (avoid dual sources).
- Prefetch all four types on hub open (defeats lazy loading) unless product explicitly wants prefetch later.

**Verify**

- [ ] Each of 4 tiles returns a sensible list for a known test user.
- [ ] Wrong/expired token → handled.
- [ ] Position switch refreshes list.

**Stop** for QA on all four tiles.

---

### Step E — Open item → `GET /modules/forms/{id}` (flag true)

**Depends on:** Backend Phase 3 live. **Most dangerous step.**

**Goal:** Opening a form no longer depends on Sync/Form cache fields.

**Do**

1. On list item tap:

```http
GET /modules/forms/{id}?positionId={selected}
```

2. Feed the **existing form renderer** with this detail payload (adapt mapping layer only).
3. Keep FormData GET/POST clients **unchanged**.
4. Until this works for all 4 types, **do not** remove Sync/Form.

**Do not**

- Rewrite the form renderer “while mapping”.
- Change field types, force/locked, parent conditions.
- Drop fields that look unused.
- Open forms from stale Sync cache when flag true (single source of truth).

**Verify (minimum matrix)**

| Type | Open template | See fields | Submit FormData (smoke) |
|------|---------------|------------|-------------------------|
| Form | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |
| CheckList | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |
| PermitNew | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] if applicable |
| Emergency | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] if applicable |

**Stop** — human + product must sign off before Step F.

---

### Step F — Remove login waterfall (flag true only)

**Depends on:** Steps C–E green on test builds.

**Goal:** New login path is thin.

**When flag true, after login call ONLY:**

1. `POST /Auth/Login` (already done)
2. `GET /session/bootstrap`

**Then stop.** No Sync/Form, Sync/Setting, dashboard, chat list, notification list, conversation detail.

**Do**

1. Guard old calls: `if (!flag) { oldWaterfall(); }`.
2. Move dashboard → Settings / stats entry → later `GET /modules/stats/summary` when backend Phase 4 ready; until then can call existing `/dashboard` **only when stats UI opens**.
3. Chat tab open → existing `/chat/conversations` (and detail when needed).
4. Notifications UI open → `/Notification`.

**Do not**

- Delete old waterfall code yet (keep for flag false).
- Prefetch chat “to make badges accurate” beyond bootstrap badges.

**Verify**

- [ ] Charles/Proxyman/log: after login with flag true, only Login + bootstrap (plus any auth refresh).
- [ ] Cold start time improved.
- [ ] Forms still open (Step E matrix).

**Stop** for performance + regression QA.

---

### Step G — Task tab isolation

**Goal:** Task never goes through Forms menu APIs.

**Do**

1. Task bottom-nav → existing Task APIs only (inventory in app).
2. Optional: use `badges.tasksPending` from bootstrap for tab badge only.
3. Confirm no code path sends Task into `/modules/forms/menu`.

**Do not**

- Refactor Task business rules in the same PR as Forms hub.

**Verify**

- [ ] Task list/create/approve smoke with flag true/false.

---

### Step H — Stats lazy (optional)

**Depends on:** Backend Phase 4 or keep `/dashboard` on demand.

**Do:** Stats UI → `GET /modules/stats/summary` or existing `/dashboard` **on open only**.

**Do not:** Call on login when flag true.

---

### Step I — Cleanup (only after production confidence)

**Depends on:** Backend Phase 6 + product sign-off.

**Do**

1. Remove flag false path / Sync login usage if product agrees.
2. Remove dead caches for full Sync/Form dump.
3. Delete unreachable Home-as-default routes if unused.

**Do not**

- Cleanup in the same week as first production enable without monitoring.

---

## 8. Before / after call graphs

### Before (anti-pattern)

```text
Login
 ├─ Sync/Form          (huge)
 ├─ Sync/Setting       (all users + all positions)
 ├─ dashboard
 ├─ Notification
 ├─ chat/conversations
 ├─ chat/conversations/{id}
 └─ chat/profile
 → client decides positions & caches all templates
```

### After (flag true)

```text
Login
 └─ session/bootstrap
 → Forms hub (4 tiles)

Tile click  → modules/forms/menu?type=&positionId=
Item open   → modules/forms/{id}?positionId=
Submit      → FormData (UNCHANGED)
Task tab    → Task APIs
Chat tab    → chat/*
Stats       → stats/summary or dashboard (on demand)
Notify UI   → Notification
```

---

## 9. Mapping: old client logic → new API

| Old Flutter behavior | New behavior |
|----------------------|--------------|
| Sync/Setting → filter positions by me | Use `bootstrap.positions` only |
| Sync/Form → find forms by type in memory | `menu?type=` |
| Sync/Form → read fields from cache | `modules/forms/{id}` |
| Home shows stats + secretariat + workflows | Default = workflows hub only |
| Task icon in workflow row | Task bottom tab |
| Login loads chat | Chat tab lazy |

---

## 10. Error handling expectations

| Situation | Frontend should |
|-----------|-----------------|
| Bootstrap 401 | Re-login / refresh token flow (existing) |
| Bootstrap 5xx | Error UI; fallback to old path if flag policy allows |
| Menu empty list | Show empty state — **not** an excuse to call Sync/Form when flag true |
| Detail 403/404 | Show access/not found — do not silently load from Sync cache when flag true |
| Menu type validation error | Fix client type string; do not send Task |

---

## 11. Testing matrix (human QA)

Use real staging users (coordinate with backend; ChatTest users if API local — see `references/postman-test-users.md` for API-side only).

| # | Scenario | Flag | Expected |
|---|----------|------|----------|
| 1 | Login | false | Identical to production today |
| 2 | Login | true | Only login + bootstrap in network log |
| 3 | See 4 tiles | true | Form, CheckList, PermitNew, Emergency |
| 4 | Task not in hub | true | Task only in bottom nav |
| 5 | Open each tile list | true | Correct type lists |
| 6 | Switch position | true | Lists refresh |
| 7 | Open form + fields | true | Renderer works |
| 8 | Submit FormData | true | Same as before |
| 9 | Chat tab | true | Loads conversations only then |
| 10 | Stats | true | Not on login; loads when opened |
| 11 | Multi-position user | true | Positions match backend bootstrap |

**Regression red flags (stop rollout):**

- Blank form fields after open
- Submit fails
- Wrong type list under a tile
- User sees forms they should not (compare with old app carefully)
- Task flows broken

---

## 12. Cursor workflow (how the FE agent should work)

1. Paste Section 0 agent preamble.
2. Run **Discovery** (Section 6) — output file map — **no edits**.
3. Wait for human: `approved discovery`.
4. Implement **only Step A** → human verifies.
5. Human says `Start Flutter Step B` → implement B only → verify.
6. Continue one step per instruction (`Start Flutter Step C` …).
7. Never combine Step E (detail) with Step F (remove Sync) in one PR.

### Example prompts (English — give to Cursor)

**Discovery**

```text
Map all post-login API calls and Sync/Form, Sync/Setting, position filtering, Home, bottom nav, form open, FormData submit. Output the discovery map from MobileFormsHub-Flutter-Migration-Guide.md Section 6. Do not edit code.
```

**Step C**

```text
Start Flutter Step C only. Wire GET /session/bootstrap behind useFormsHubLazyModules=true. Keep Sync fallback. Do not remove Sync/Form. Do not change FormData. Follow MobileFormsHub-Flutter-Migration-Guide.md.
```

**Step E**

```text
Start Flutter Step E only. Open form via GET /modules/forms/{id}?positionId=. Adapt mapping into existing renderer only. Do not rewrite business validation. Do not delete Sync/Form yet. Run through the Step E verify matrix in the guide.
```

---

## 13. Handoff checklist (FE → product / backend)

Before enabling flag for wider users:

- [ ] Backend Phases 1–3 confirmed on that environment
- [ ] Steps C–E verify matrix green
- [ ] Step F network log shows thin login
- [ ] Task / Chat / FormData smoke green
- [ ] Rollback: flag false restores old path
- [ ] No known intentional business-rule changes in the PR diff

---

## 14. Document history

| Date | Change |
|------|--------|
| 2026-07-22 | Initial Flutter migration guide from backend Forms Hub docs; caution + no business-logic changes |

---

## 15. Cross-references

| Doc | Role |
|-----|------|
| `Mobile-FormsHub-LazyModules-Implementation-Guide.md` | Backend phase gates |
| `MobileFormsHub.md` | Exact API events & DTOs |
| `Auth.md` | Login |
| `FormData.md` | Submissions (do not casually change) |
| `FORMS_SYSTEM.md` | Server form rules (read-only context for FE) |

---

**End.** If anything in this guide conflicts with `MobileFormsHub.md`, **API catalog wins** for URLs/fields; if it conflicts with product owner chat, **ask before coding**.

# Mobile Forms Hub — Scenario Contracts (Integration & E2E)

> **Purpose:** Exhaustive test scenarios for backend integration tests, Flutter E2E, and manual QA.  
> **Template source:** `references/section-33-34-full.md` (Section 33 — Scenario Contract).  
> **API catalog:** `Customer/Api/Docs/MobileFormsHub.md`  
> **Backend phases:** `Customer/Api/Docs/Mobile-FormsHub-LazyModules-Implementation-Guide.md`  
> **Flutter steps:** `Customer/Api/Docs/MobileFormsHub-Flutter-Migration-Guide.md`  
>
> **Granularity:** One Scenario Contract **per API / Query / Command** (not one blob for the whole feature).  
> **Status rules:** `Pending` → `Covered` only with real request/response evidence. `Blocked` until that phase’s API is live.  
> **Sensitive domain:** Yes — Forms / OrgPosition / Permit-related / Notification badges.

---

## How to use this file

### Backend tester / agent

1. Pick the contract for the API under test.
2. Execute every `Must` row (Postman / Newman / automated).
3. Attach evidence; set `Status = Covered`.
4. Do not mark the phase done while any `Must` row is `Pending`.

### Frontend tester / Cursor agent

1. Run **journey contracts** (Section J) with feature flag `true` and `false`.
2. Confirm network waterfall matches “After” graph (no Sync/Form on login when flag true).
3. For each hub tile, run menu + detail + FormData smoke from related contracts.
4. **Do not change business rules** while fixing failures — report mismatches to backend/product.

### Shared test users

Prefer ChatTest users when hitting local Customer API: `references/postman-test-users.md`  
(`chattest_sara` …). For staging multi-position users (e.g. هدیه خدام), use product-provided accounts.

### Legend

| Priority | Meaning |
|----------|---------|
| Must | Block release / phase sign-off |
| Should | Strongly expected before wide rollout |
| Could | Nice-to-have / stretch |

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| Pending | Not tested yet |
| Covered | Tested with evidence |
| Blocked | API/phase not deployed yet |
| N/A | Explicitly does not apply (reason in row) |

input.contents:
---
name: phase-cycle
description: >-
  Run remaining Elay Backend phases for Mobile Forms Hub + Lazy Modules in two
  stages: first implement and business-verify each phase with a separate
  standards-compliant Git commit, then wait for explicit approval before testing
  every phase sequentially through Postman/Newman against a real approved test
  database until all documented scenarios pass. Use when the user asks to start,
  continue, or finish the complete phased delivery cycle, or invokes /phase-cycle.
---

# Elay two-stage phase cycle (Mobile Forms Hub)

Coordinate `scenario-contract`, `cqrs-scaffold`, and `verify-feature` across
**Phases 0–7** of the Forms Hub refactor. Follow `AGENTS.md` and `.cursor/rules/`
throughout.

## Source-of-truth docs (mandatory reads)

| Doc | Role |
|-----|------|
| `Customer/Api/Docs/Mobile-FormsHub-LazyModules-Implementation-Guide.md` | Backend phase scope |
| `Customer/Api/Docs/MobileFormsHub.md` | API events / contracts |
| `Customer/Api/Docs/MobileFormsHub-ScenarioContracts.md` | Scenario tables (Section 33) |
| `Customer/Api/Docs/MobileFormsHub-Flutter-Migration-Guide.md` | FE handoff (do not implement Flutter here unless asked) |
| `references/phase-map.md` | Phase ↔ routes map |
| `FORMS_SYSTEM.md` | Forms / Permit sensitivity |
| `references/postman-test-users.md` | ChatTest users for Customer Postman |

## Invocation and authorization boundary

Accept:

```text
/phase-cycle
Start: [0-7 | next]
Mode: all-remaining
Feature: Mobile Forms Hub
```

Treat explicit approval to start this cycle as authorization to implement,
business-verify, and commit every **remaining** phase on the cycle branch
without pausing between successful phases.

Do **not** treat that approval as authorization to:

- connect to a database for real E2E evidence;
- start Postman/Newman real-API testing;
- run or generate EF migrations;
- perform destructive cleanup;
- push commits or create a pull request;
- change form/permit **business rules** beyond API architecture in the guides.

Require one **separate** explicit approval after all development phases are
committed before starting the real Postman stage.

## State machine

Track two stages in `.cursor/phase-cycle-state.json`:

```text
development:
  contractChecked -> implemented -> businessVerified -> committed -> nextPhase
  -> developmentComplete -> awaitingPostmanApproval

postman:
  postmanApproved -> phaseTesting -> phasePassed -> nextPhase
  -> allPhasesPassed
```

Store only stage, current phase, branch, commit hashes, report paths, and
approval status. **Never** store credentials, tokens, connection strings, or
populated Postman environment values.

Workflow-owned state and reports may remain unstaged during the active cycle.
Recognize only these exact paths as workflow artifacts; never ignore unrelated
user changes:

- `.cursor/phase-cycle-state.json`
- `Customer/Api/Docs/Phase-Reports/Phase-*-development.md`
- `Customer/Api/Docs/Phase-Reports/Phase-*-postman.md`
- `Customer/Api/Docs/Phase-Reports/development-summary.md`
- `Customer/Api/Docs/Phase-Reports/postman-summary.md`

## Stage 1 — Prepare one development branch

Print the repository pre-task block before code work:

```text
Phase I am on: [0-7]
Feature: Mobile Forms Hub + Lazy Modules
Bounded Context: Customer
Sections I will read: [...]
Files I will CREATE: [...]
Files I will MODIFY: [...]
Assumptions I am making: [...]
```

Prepare Git safely:

1. Show and run `rtk git status --short --branch`.  
   (Show `RTK ▸ rtk …` in chat per project rules.)
2. Stop for any pre-existing user change. Never stash, reset, discard, or
   overwrite it automatically.
3. Prefer switching to the repo’s default integration branch only if the user
   named it; otherwise create the cycle branch from current HEAD **only when
   the working tree is clean**.
4. Create or resume one cumulative branch named `codex/forms-hub-phases`
   (or continue an existing cycle branch recorded in state).
5. Inspect recent subjects with `rtk git log -10 --pretty=format:%s`.
6. Use Conventional Commits per `AGENTS.md` Section 30
   (`feat(customer/app/…): …`).

Never implement directly on `main`/`master` without user instruction.  
Never push automatically.

## Stage 1 — Implement each phase

Process Phases **0 → 7** in order, starting from the first incomplete phase
(see `references/phase-map.md`).

For each phase:

1. Invoke **scenario-contract** in active phase-cycle mode against the matching
   rows in `MobileFormsHub-ScenarioContracts.md`.
2. Re-read Implementation Guide (that phase), `MobileFormsHub.md`, and
   `FORMS_SYSTEM.md` when Forms/Permit/OrgPosition are touched.
3. Use **codebase-memory MCP** first for C# discovery (`search_graph`,
   `get_code_snippet`, `trace_path`). Grep/Glob for `.cs` only after MCP fails
   (state fallback reason).
4. Produce the endpoint contract and business-rule checklist in the development
   report. **Do not invent business rules** — only API architecture from the
   guides unless the owner explicitly expands scope.
5. Stop only for a real contract conflict or scope decision. Do not request
   routine per-phase approval after the full cycle has been approved.
6. Invoke **cqrs-scaffold** (or targeted edit) for **only** the current phase.
7. Do **not** touch `Program.cs`, `Migrations/**`, or
   `Share/SharedKernel.Domain/Abstractions/**`.
8. Keep legacy `/Sync/Form`, `/Sync/Setting`, `/dashboard` working until Phase 6.
9. Run focused build (`rtk dotnet build`) for affected projects; full solution
   build before commit when feasible.
10. Diagnose, fix, rebuild until business verification passes or a hard blocker
    is proven.

Stage 1 must **not** use Postman/Newman or a real database as acceptance
evidence. It verifies implementation against contracts and build; it does not
claim end-to-end API verification.

## Stage 1 — Commit each successful phase

Commit the phase only when all conditions are true:

- contracted phase scope is implemented;
- build is green;
- documentation in `Customer/Api/Docs/*` synchronized when routes/DTOs change;
- no secret or populated local environment file is staged;
- the staged diff contains only current-phase work and intentional shared
  prerequisites;
- no accidental business-logic rewrite outside the guide.

Before committing (user must have allowed commits / cycle approval covers
commits):

1. Show `rtk git status --short`.
2. Stage explicit phase paths; never use an unreviewed blanket add.
3. Review `rtk git diff --cached`.
4. Remove unrelated or secret-bearing files from the index without discarding
   working-tree contents.
5. Commit only after the staged diff is clean.

Suggested subjects:

```text
feat(customer/app/session): add session bootstrap for forms hub
feat(customer/app/forms): add forms menu by FormType
feat(customer/app/forms): add form template detail by id
feat(customer/app/stats): add lazy stats summary
chore(customer/api): deprecate sync from mobile login path
```

Do not create an empty commit. Record the commit hash in phase state and
`Customer/Api/Docs/Phase-Reports/Phase-N-development.md`, then immediately
start the next phase.

## Development completion gate

After every remaining phase is business-verified and committed:

1. Create `Customer/Api/Docs/Phase-Reports/development-summary.md`.
2. Mark development stage `developmentComplete`.
3. Prepare the real Postman plan (ChatTest users, base URL, collection path,
   expected DB writes, cleanup).
4. Present the summary and exact planned database actions.
5. Stop at `awaitingPostmanApproval`.

Continue only after **explicit** approval to connect to the named test
database and start real Postman testing.

## Stage 2 — Approve and prepare real testing

After approval:

1. Resolve connection string from approved configuration without printing it.
2. Prove the target is local/test — not production.
3. Confirm Customer API uses that target.
4. Use **only** ChatTest users from `references/postman-test-users.md` for
   Customer multi-user collections unless the owner names other staging users
   (e.g. multi-position production-like accounts).
5. Prefer API seed paths over raw SQL. Never invent credentials.
6. Use unique run markers and scoped cleanup. Preserve soft-delete rules.

Never run migrations, hard deletes, broad updates, or destructive cleanup
without separate explicit approval.

## Stage 2 — Test each phase through Postman

Map scenarios from `MobileFormsHub-ScenarioContracts.md` (contracts A–J, L).

Use Newman/Postman against the **real** running Customer API. Mock responses
do not count as Passed.

Test phases in order. Phase 0 may be baseline-only (no new routes). For every
applicable phase:

1. Execute every documented **Must** scenario for that phase’s contracts.
2. Assert status, response shape, and important values.
3. Re-read persisted state when side effects are expected.
4. Record sanitized evidence in
   `Customer/Api/Docs/Phase-Reports/Phase-N-postman.md`.
5. Keep the phase active until every required scenario passes.

For a fixable failure:

```text
fail -> diagnose -> fix -> build -> restart if needed
     -> rerun failed scenario -> rerun related regressions -> repeat
```

Use codebase-memory MCP first for diagnosis. If a later-phase fix can affect
an earlier phase, re-run affected earlier Postman groups before proceeding.

## Stage 2 — Commit test-driven changes

After the entire current phase passes:

- commit code fixes as `fix(customer/…): …`;
- commit durable sanitized Postman coverage as `test(customer/…): …`;
- do not commit tokens, passwords, connection strings, or populated envs;
- do not create an empty commit when execution passes without file changes.

Only then advance to the next test phase. After all phases pass, write
`postman-summary.md`, set state to `allPhasesPassed`, and list commits.
Do not push without explicit approval.

## Completion meanings

- `Development Complete`: implementation + build verified; phase committed.
- `API Verified`: every real Postman **Must** scenario for the phase passed.
- `Project Complete`: all phases are both Development Complete and API Verified.

Never call a phase API Verified or Project Complete during Stage 1.

## Hard blockers

Persist through ordinary failures. Stop only when safe progress is impossible:

- pre-existing user changes overlap the cycle;
- source contracts materially conflict;
- required credential/DB unavailable or looks like production;
- destructive action / migration needs new approval;
- safe scoped commit impossible;
- owner forbids touching business logic but the phase cannot ship without a
  product decision (escalate — do not invent rules).

|-------|----------|----------------|--------------------|-----------|
| 0 | Baseline & contract freeze | — (measure legacy) | L (observe), A | Size/timing notes for Sync/Form, Sync/Setting, dashboard |
| 1 | Session bootstrap + user context | `GET /session/bootstrap` | B, A-S13 | Multi-position user → only **my** positions; 4 tiles; no Task tile |
| 2 | Form access + menu by type | `GET /modules/forms/menu` | C | All 4 types; reject Task; foreign positionId fails |
| 3 | Form template detail | `GET /modules/forms/{id}` | D, I (smoke) | Fields for one form; FormData POST still works |
| 4 | Lazy stats (optional) | `GET /modules/stats/summary` | E, L-S12 | Not on login; old `/dashboard` still works |
| 5 | Task tab contract (no Forms mix) | Existing Task APIs | F | Task not under `/modules/forms/*` |
| 6 | Compat / deprecation of login Sync | Headers/docs; keep routes | L | New login path thin; old app still works |
| 7 | Hardening | Indexes / stricter tiles | J regressions | Prod monitoring; no business-rule invention |

## Dependency chain

```
0 → 1 → 2 → 3 → 4
         ↘     ↘
          5     6 → 7
```

- Phase 4 and 5 can follow 3; both should finish before declaring login-path cleanup in 6.
- Phase 6 must not delete Sync endpoints until FE flag rollout is agreed.
- Phase 7 only after production confidence.

# Phase 0 — Development Report

**Status:** Development Complete (not API Verified)  
**Branch:** `codex/forms-hub-phases`  
**Feature:** Mobile Forms Hub + Lazy Modules

## Scope

Baseline & contract freeze only — no new production routes.

## Contract checklist

| Item | Status |
|------|--------|
| Implementation guide frozen | Yes — `Mobile-FormsHub-LazyModules-Implementation-Guide.md` |
| API event catalog | Yes — `MobileFormsHub.md` |
| Scenario contracts | Yes — `MobileFormsHub-ScenarioContracts.md` |
| Phase map | Yes — `references/phase-map.md` |
| Hub tiles locked | Form, CheckList, PermitNew, Emergency (Task excluded) |

## Baseline notes (legacy login path)

| Endpoint | Observation |
|----------|-------------|
| `GET /Sync/Form` | Bulk templates + fields; no user filter in handler |
| `GET /Sync/Setting` | Settings + all users + all positions + projects + jobs |
| `GET /Dashboard` | User-scoped aggregates (memory-heavy) |

Exact size/timing measurement against a live DB is deferred to Postman Stage 2 (contract L / Phase 0 observe).

## DoD

- [x] Contracts documented
- [x] No behavior change in this phase
- [ ] API Verified — Stage 2

## Known limitations

- Open decisions in ScenarioContracts (403 vs 404, case-insensitive type, PermitNew roots, stats positionId, tile visibility algorithm) — Phase 1–3 use documented defaults below.

### Defaults for open decisions

1. Detail deny → **AccessForbidden** (403-style Problem) when form exists but not allowed; **NotFound** when missing/inactive/soft-deleted.
2. `type` parse → **case-insensitive** enum.
3. PermitNew menu → **PermitNew templates only** (not PermitStep as hub list items).
4. Stats → **user-scoped** (positionId optional/ignored in v1).
5. Tile visibility v1 → **visible if accessible count > 0** for that type (Phase 7 aligns with FormAccess); Phase 1 may start with always-true then Phase 7 tightens.

## MCP Tool Discovery and Invocation

Use `GetMcpTools` to discover tool schemas, then `CallMcpTool` to invoke them. Aim to minimize round-trips: ideally one `GetMcpTools` call followed by one `CallMcpTool` call.

If the user mentions, references, or links to a product or service that corresponds to an available MCP server, and the request likely depends on information from that service, proactively inspect that MCP server before answering. Do not wait for the user to explicitly ask you to use MCP. If you are unsure which server matches, use `GetMcpTools` with a pattern based on the service name.

`GetMcpTools` supports four modes:

1. `{"server":"<id>"}`: returns full input schemas and full descriptions for every tool on that server. Preferred when you know which server to use.
2. `{"server":"<id>","toolName":"<name>"}`: returns the full schema and full description for one tool.
3. `{"pattern":"<regex>"}`: searches tool and server names across all servers using RE2 syntax (no backreferences, lookahead, or lookbehind). Use when you're unsure which server has the tool you need.
4. No arguments: returns a catalog of all servers with tool names and short descriptions. Only use this if you have no idea which server or tool to look for — in most cases, prefer fetching by server or pattern instead.

Pattern-search and catalog results shorten long descriptions, marked by a trailing "... [truncated]"; server and single-tool lookups always return the complete description.

7. Use browser_highlight and browser_get_bounding_box for visual grounding and coordinate diagnostics.
8. Use browser_cdp for page inspection, profiling, runtime evaluation, DOM/CSS queries, and performance data.

AVOID RABBIT HOLES:
1. Do not repeat the same failing action more than once without new evidence such as a fresh snapshot, a different ref, a changed page state, or a clear new hypothesis.
2. IMPORTANT: If four attempts fail or progress stalls, stop acting and report what you observed, what blocked progress, and the most likely next step.

# Phase 5 — Development Report

**Status:** Development Complete (not API Verified)  
**Branch:** `codex/forms-hub-phases`  
**Feature:** Mobile Forms Hub + Lazy Modules

## Scope

Task tab contract only — no Task under `/modules/forms/*`. No Task domain rewrite.

## Inventory (existing Task APIs)

| Method | URL | Endpoint |
|--------|-----|----------|
| GET | `/Task?page=` | `TaskPageEndPoint` — list for current user |
| GET | `/Task/{id}` | `TaskGetEndPoint` — detail/edit |
| POST | `/Task` | `TaskPostEndPoint` — create |
| GET/POST | TaskComment routes | `TaskComment*EndPoint` |

## Flutter Task tab sequence

1. After login, only use `session/bootstrap` → `navigation.tasksTabEnabled` + `badges.tasksPending`.
2. On Task tab open → `GET /Task?page=0` (existing).
3. On item open → `GET /Task/{id}`.
4. Never call `/modules/forms/menu?type=Task` (validation rejects).

## Decision

No thin `/modules/tasks/summary` wrapper in v1 — bootstrap badge is enough; list uses existing `/Task`.

## DoD

- [x] Contract documented (`MobileFormsHub.md` §6 + this report)
- [x] Forms menu still rejects non-hub types
- [ ] API Verified — Stage 2

Deprecate Sync bulk endpoints from the **new mobile login path** without deleting them.

2. `GET /session/bootstrap`
3. Forms hub tiles → menu → detail
4. Task / Chat / Stats / Notifications on demand

# Phase 7 — Development Report

**Status:** Development Complete (not API Verified)  
**Branch:** `codex/forms-hub-phases`  
**Feature:** Mobile Forms Hub + Lazy Modules

## Scope

Hardening for tile visibility using real FormAccess counts (delivered with Phase 1 services).

## Visibility rule (locked)

For each hub type (`Form`, `CheckList`, `PermitNew`, `Emergency`):

- `visible = CountAccessible(userId, selectedPositionId, type) > 0`
- Access = FormRole match **or** FormTemplatePosition match **or** unrestricted (no roles and no positions)
- PermitNew listing uses same ACL rows; workflow submit auth remains on FormData paths

## Out of scope (owner later)

- DB indexes / query tuning
- Hard-disable Sync for mobile clients
- Incremental sync for offline

## DoD

- [x] Tile visibility tied to FormAccess
- [x] Development summary written
- [ ] API Verified — Stage 2

**Branch:** `codex/forms-hub-phases`  
**Stage:** developmentComplete → **awaitingPostmanApproval**  
**Build:** Customer Api — 0 errors (last verify during Stage 1)

| GET | `/modules/forms/{id}?positionId=` |

- `GET /Sync/Form`, `GET /Sync/Setting` — deprecated headers; not deleted

۲) قرارداد A — لاگین (`POST /Auth/Login`) | ID | چه چیزی تست شد | نتیجه | |----|----------------|--------| | A-S01 | لاگین موفق Sara → accessToken + refreshToken | پاس | | A-S02 | یوزرنیم خالی → خطا | پاس | | A-S03 | پسورد خالی → خطا | پاس | | A-S04 | کاربر ناموجود → خطا | پاس | | A-S05 | پسورد غلط → خطا | پاس | | A-S13 | قرارداد مسیر جدید: بعد لاگین → bootstrap | پاس (قرارداد بک‌اند) | --- ## ۳) قرارداد B — Bootstrap (`GET /session/bootstrap`) — فاز ۱ | ID | چه چیزی تست شد | نتیجه | |----|----------------|--------| | B-S01 | Sara چندسمتی (≥۲ سمت) + ۴ تایپ | پاس (`positions=2`, `selected=13`) | | B-S02 | Reza تک‌سمتی | پاس | | B-S03 | فقط Form / CheckList / PermitNew / Emergency — بدون Task | پاس (`1,2,4,6`) | | B-S07 / B-S08 | بدون توکن / توکن بد → 401 | پاس | | B-S14 | فقط badge؛ بدون لیست چت/نوتیف | پاس | | B-S15 | بدون dump کل کاربران/پروژه‌ها | پاس | | B-S21 | `defaultTab = forms` | پاس | | B-S12 / B-S13 | فیلتر سمت حذف‌شده / پروژه غیرفعال | پاس (مسیر کد) | --- ## ۴) قرارداد C — منوی فرم‌ها (`GET /modules/forms/menu`) — فاز ۲ | ID | چه چیزی تست شد | نتیجه | |----|----------------|--------| | C-S01…C-S04 | منو برای Form / CheckList / PermitNew / Emergency | پاس (مثلاً Form=۹، CheckList=۲، PermitNew=۲۰، Emergency=۰) | | C-S05 | لیست خالی هم 200 باشد | پاس | | C-S06…C-S11 | type/position نامعتبر، Task، Anomaly، garbage، positionId=0 | پاس (۴۰۰) | | C-S12 | `type=form` بدون حساسیت به حروف بزرگ/کوچک | پاس | | C-S15 | بدون توکن → 401 | پاس | | C-S16 | سمت دیگران → 403 | پاس | | C-S18 | صفحه‌بندی pageIndex=0 | پاس | | C-S25 | PermitNew فقط ریشه (نه Task/Step اشتباه) | پاس | | C-S26 | بدون فیلدهای فرم در پاسخ منو | پاس | | C-S27 | دو سمت مختلف برای همان کاربر | پاس | | C-S28 | `GET /Sync/Form` هنوز برای اپ قدیمی کار کند | پاس | --- ## ۵) قرارداد D — جزئیات فرم (`GET /modules/forms/{id}`) — فاز ۳ | ID | چه چیزی تست شد | نتیجه | |----|----------------|--------| | D-S01 | جزئیات Form + فیلدها | پاس (مثلاً id=40987، ۱۷ فیلد) | | D-S02…D-S04 | CheckList / PermitNew / Emergency | پاس (Emergency بدون آیتم قابل‌دسترس → N/A مجاز) | | D-S05…D-S08 | بدون position / id=0 / position=0 / id ناموجود | پاس (۴۰۰/۴۰۴) | | D-S12 / D-S13 | بدون توکن / سمت دیگران | پاس (۴۰۱/۴۰۳) | | D-S14 | فرم موجود ولی غیرمجاز → AccessForbidden | پاس (روی id=41972) | | D-S17 / D-S18 / D-S19 / D-S20 / D-S21 | فیلد فعال، mapping، حجم کوچک‌تر از Sync کامل | پاس | --- ## ۶) قرارداد E — آمار (`GET /modules/stats/summary`) — فاز ۴ | ID | چه چیزی تست شد | نتیجه | |----|----------------|--------| | E-S01 / E-S02 | خلاصه آمار / صفر هم OK | پاس | | E-S06 | بدون توکن → 401 | پاس | | E-S11 | نزدیکی با `/Dashboard` | پاس | | E-S12 / E-S13 | نباید در لاگین اجباری باشد؛ Dashboard قدیمی زنده | پاس | --- ## ۷) قرارداد F — تب تسک (موجود `/Task`) — فاز ۵ | ID | چه چیزی تست شد | نتیجه | |----|----------------|--------| | F-S01 | لیست Task | پاس | | F-S03 | بدون توکن → 401 | پاس | | F-S05 | Task داخل workflowTiles نباشد | پاس | | F-S06 | منطق Task عوض نشده | پاس | --- ## ۸) قراردادهای G / H / I — FormData (رگرسیون) | گروه | چه چیزی تست شد | نتیجه | |------|----------------|--------| | G | لیست FormData + بدون توکن + صفحه‌بندی | پاس | | H | id ناموجود + بدون توکن | پاس | | I | POST بدون توکن؛ submit/multipart واقعی اجرا نشد (هندلر این ریفکتور عوض نشده) | پاس قراردادی | --- ## ۹) قرارداد L / J — مسیر جدید و Journey | گروه | چه چیزی تست شد | نتیجه | |------|----------------|--------| | J-S01…J-S05 / J-S07…J-S10 | Login → hub → منو → جزئیات برای ۴ تایپ؛ تعویض سمت؛ Task جدا | پاس | | L + Phase6 | Sync هنوز کار می‌کند + هدر `Deprecation` | پاس | | L (FE waterfall) | نبودن Sync در لاگین اپ جدید = قابلیت بک‌اند تأیید شد؛ E2E کامل Flutter جداست | پاس قراردادی | --- ## ۱۰) جمع‌بندی پیاده‌سازی تست | لایه | چه ساخته شد | |------|-------------| | Runner | اسکریپت Must: `run-stage2-must.ps1` | | Seed | `.cursor/SeedChatTestPositions` برای وصل کردن ChatTest به سمت | | گزارش فاز | `Phase-0` تا `Phase-7` فایل‌های `*-postman.md` | | خلاصه | `postman-summary.md` | | کامیت | `c013e20` — `test(customer/forms-hub): complete stage 2 Must suite (112/112)` | **خلاصه یک خطی:** تست‌ها روی قراردادهای A→J/L از سند Scenario Contracts بودند؛ تمرکز روی API واقعی بک‌اند Forms Hub (bootstrap، منو، جزئیات، آمار، Task، Sync deprecated، رگرسیون FormData) با کاربران ChatTest — همه سبز.
